Semantic Scholar, ArXiv) startet und einen Novelty-Score basierend auf Textähnlichkeit und Metadaten zurückgibt; Kandidaten unter einem Schwellwert werden als Wiederentdeckung markiert und nicht weiterverfolgt.", "acceptance": "Mindestens 90% der von menschlichen Experten als Wiederentdeckung identifizierten Kandidaten werden vom Gate erkannt; false-positive Rate unter 10%."}
message.reasoning_content: We are asked: "Could this source concretely improve exactly one of those modules without touching the protected core?" The source is about "rediscovery risk" in bounded AutoResearch, where a system might generate locally valid but not novel outputs. The allowed modules are non-core. The source describes a problem: AutoResearch might misclassify novelty. It suggests "literature-positioning gates" and "expert judgment" to mitigate rediscovery risk. The modules include "semantics-measurement" (projector and candidate extraction), "conflict-qualifier" (markers that name conflict kind), "reader-sources" (reading layer, which feeds Joni can draw on), "emergence" (how candidates earn development), "method-trialing" (pass-criterion and retirement of methods). The source might help reader-sources by suggesting ways to integrate literature-positioning gates to check novelty. Reader-sources module is about which feeds Joni can draw on; so adding a check against existing literature to avoid rediscovery could improve that module. That is a concrete change: incorporate a literature position gate that queries external knowledge bases to assess novelty of a candidate before development. So it maps to reader-sources. Other modules don't fit as well. Conflict-qualifier is about naming conflict kind, not about novelty. Semantics-measurement is about projection, not novelty. Emergence is about how candidates earn development, but the source suggests a gate that could be part of emergence or reader-sources; but reader-sources is about feeding sources, so incorporating external literature search would be a source. The motivation: the source warns that AutoResearch can produce locally valid but not novel results; by integrating a literature-positioning gate, we reduce rediscovery risk. The desired change: in reader-sources, add a step that, for each candidate, queries a curated set of knowledge sources to check if it's already known, partially known, or adjacent, and if so, flag it or adjust novelty score. Acceptance criterion: when a candidate is truly novel, it should pass the gate; when it's a rediscovery, the gate should catch it with high recall. Semantic Scholar, ArXiv) startet und einen Novelty-Score basierend auf Textähnlichkeit und Metadaten zurückgibt; Kandidaten unter einem Schwellwert werden als Wiederentdeckung markiert und nicht weiterverfolgt.", "acceptance": "Mindestens 90% der von menschlichen Experten als Wiederentdeckung identifizierten Kandidaten werden vom Gate erkannt; false-positive Rate unter 10%."}.
